evidence: "This series provides for the Anglophile another segment of English history from 1485-1603."
"I enjoyed the two films because I am an incurable sci-fi nut."
"The film has a clear story line."
"The story becomes less a reverse parody of 'Frankenstein' as it presents itself as a satire on who is the more human."
"The writing, cinematography, acting, and storyline up to the climax is worthy of 5 stars."
"I do not like black and white films, films that hinder a clear view of the characters through many scenes filmed in almost total darkness."
"The film provides yet another twist on the vampire tale: this time with a Dr. Ravner as the sharp toothed prince of darkness."
"The Archive Collection does a superb job of restoring the films it puts on DVD so that the viewer, no doubt, sees them better than those who viewed them 40 years ago."
"I remember seeing these Dr. David Sorel (Louis Jordan) supernatural thrillers when they first aired as TV movies."
"I heartily recommend this series as well as universal DVD disk players."
"The film has interest to the 1970s enthusiast, who doesn't mind bawdiness."
"The prospect of forcing evil to destroy itself is intriguing."
"I gave the film five stars because it deeply moved me."
"Beautiful story! Angelica Houston is ravishing! I highly recommend this film."

summary: The user has a strong preference for historical dramas and sci-fi, often appreciating high production quality and emotional depth in storytelling. They enjoy narratives that evoke strong feelings and are likely to appreciate well-crafted books that explore similar themes.
